content.text:
You are doing a **Standards-axis** code review of an uncommitted diff in the Rust repo at `/Users/mseele/dev/sve/sve-backend`.

Fixed point: commit `1deb7d4` (the merge-base / branch tip before the work). The diff is the working-tree changes against that commit. There are no commits yet — the work exists only as edits.

Command to retrieve the full diff:
```
git diff 1deb7d4
```
Run it from the repo root with `SQLX_OFFLINE=true` if you cargo anything.

Per the implement/spec history, this refactor (GitHub issue #77 "Extract events::notifications into its own module to improve notification tests"):
- Splits `src/logic/events.rs` (2351 LOC) into `src/logic/events/mod.rs` (remaining ~14 functions) and `src/logic/events/notifications.rs` (4 notification functions: `send_event_email`, `send_event_reminders`, `send_payment_reminders`, `send_participation_confirmation` + the `create_prebooking_link` helper).
- Updates call sites in `src/api.rs` and `src/logic/tasks.rs` to `events::notifications::*`.
- Moves notification tests next to `notifications.rs`.
- Lifts the shared test helpers `mock_gateway_discarding` and `mock_gateway_times` from `events.rs`'s test mod into `src/test_utils.rs` as `pub(crate)`.
- Adjusts `include_str!` paths (one dir deeper).

## Standards sources in this repo

- `AGENTS.md` at the repo root — the engineering standards file. Read it. It documents:
  - Import order: `std::`, external crates, `crate::` (blank lines between groups).
  - `pub(crate)` for crate-wide items. PascalCase types, snake_case fns, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE constants.
  - `anyhow::Result<T>`, `?`, `bail!`, `.context()` for errors.
  - `i32` for DB IDs, `BigDecimal` for money, `DateTime<Utc>` for timestamps.
  - Compile-time checked SQLx queries; `QueryBuilder` for dynamic queries.
  - Inline `#[cfg(test)]` modules; use `pretty_assertions`.
  - Structure: Logic in `src/logic/`, models in `src/models.rs`, routes in `src/api.rs`, DB in `src/db.rs`.
- `CONTEXT.md` if present, and `docs/adr/` for ADRs.

## Smell baseline (apply on top of repo standards)

Each smell is a judgement-call heuristic; the repo's documented standards always override it. Skip anything tooling (clippy, rustfmt) already enforces — `cargo clippy --all-features` and `cargo fmt --check` are already clean on this diff.

- Mysterious Name — fn/var/type name doesn't reveal what it does.
- Duplicated Code — same logic shape appears in >1 hunk/file.
- Feature Envy — method reaches into another object's data more than its own.
- Data Clumps — same few fields/params keep travelling together.
- Primitive Obsession — primitive standing in for a domain concept.
- Repeated Switches — same `switch`/if-cascade on the same type recurs.
- Shotgun Surgery — one logical change forces scattered edits.
- Divergent Change — one file/module edited for several unrelated reasons.
- Speculative Generality — abstraction/params/hooks for needs the spec doesn't have.
- Message Chains — long `a.b().c().d()` the caller shouldn't depend on.
- Middle Man — class/fn mostly just delegates onward.
- Refused Bequest — subclass/impl ignores most of what it inherits.

## Brief

Report — per file/hunk where relevant — (a) every place the diff violates a documented standard in AGENTS.md: cite the standard (file + the rule) and quote the offending hunk; and (b) any baseline smell you spot in the new/moved code: name it and quote the hunk. Distinguish hard violations from judgement calls — documented-standard breaches can be hard, baseline smells are always judgement calls, and a documented repo standard overrides the baseline. Skip anything tooling enforces (rustfmt/clippy are already clean).

Under 400 words. Be specific — quote code. Do not edit files.

16: /// Send an event email to all bookings and/or the waiting list of an event.
17: pub(crate) async fn send_event_email(
18:     pool: &PgPool,
19:     data: EventEmail,
20:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
21: ) -> Result<()> {
22:     if !data.bookings && !data.waiting_list {
23:         bail!("Either bookings or waiting list option need to be selected to send an event email.")
24:     }
25:     let enrolled = if data.bookings && !data.waiting_list {
26:         Some(true)
27:     } else if data.waiting_list && !data.bookings {
28:         Some(false)
29:     } else {
30:         None
31:     };
32: 
33:     let bookings = db::get_bookings(pool, &data.event_id, enrolled).await?;
34:     if bookings.is_empty() {
35:         return Ok(());
36:     }
37: 
38:     // calculate correct event id
39:     let event_id = match &data.prebooking_event_id {
40:         Some(event_id) => event_id,
41:         None => &data.event_id,
42:     };
43: 
44:     let event = db::get_event(pool, event_id, false)
45:         .await?
46:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no event with id '{}'", event_id))?;
47: 
48:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
49:     let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
50:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
51: 
52:     for (booking, subscriber_id, payment_id) in bookings {
53:         let prebooking_link;
54:         if let Some(event_id) = data.prebooking_event_id {
55:             prebooking_link = Some(create_prebooking_link(
56:                 event.event_type,
57:                 event_id,
58:                 subscriber_id,
59:             )?);
60:         } else {
61:             prebooking_link = None;
62:         }
63: 
64:         let body = template::render_booking(
65:             &data.body,
66:             &booking,
67:             &event,
68:             Some(payment_id),
69:             prebooking_link,
70:             None,
71:         )?;
72: 
73:         let attachments = data
74:             .attachments
75:             .as_ref()
76:             .map(|attachments| attachments.to_vec());
77: 
78:         messages.push(
79:             Email::new(
80:                 message_type,
81:                 booking.email,
82:                 data.subject.clone(),
83:                 body,
84:                 attachments,
85:             )
86:             .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
87:         );
88:     }
89: 
90:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
91: 
92:     Ok(())
93: }
94: 
95: /// send a reminder email for each events that starts next week
96: pub(crate) async fn send_event_reminders(
97:     pool: &PgPool,
98:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
99: ) -> Result<usize> {
100:     // get all events where a event reminder should be send to the subscribers
101:     let events = db::get_reminder_events(pool).await?;
102: 
103:     // process each event
104:     for event in &events {
105:         // prepare for message generation
106:         let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
107:         let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
108:         let mut messages = Vec::new();
109: 
110:         // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
111:         let (subject, body) = match event.event_type {
112:             EventType::Fitness => (
113:                 format!("{} Info zum Kursstart", event.subject_prefix()),
114:                 include_str!("../../../templates/event_reminder_fitness.txt"),
115:             ),
116:             EventType::Events => (
117:                 format!("{} Info zum Eventstart", event.subject_prefix()),
118:                 include_str!("../../../templates/event_reminder_events.txt"),
119:             ),
120:         };
121: 
122:         // iterate all enrolled event subscribers (Option should never be None)
123:         if let Some(subscribers) = &event.subscribers {
124:             for subscriber in subscribers.iter().filter(|s| s.enrolled) {
125:                 // render the body for the email...
126:                 let body = template::render_event_reminder(body, event, subscriber)?;
127: 
128:                 // ...and push the email into the messages list
129:                 messages.push(
130:                     Email::new(
131:                         message_type,
132:                         subscriber.email.clone(),
133:                         subject.clone(),
134:                         body,
135:                         None,
136:                     )
137:                     .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
138:                 );
139:             }
140: 
141:             // send reminder emails to all event subribers
142:             email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
143: 
144:             // mark reminder has been sent to the event
145:             // (to avoid duplicate sending of reminder emails)
146:             db::mark_as_reminder_sent(pool, &event.id).await?;
147:         }
148:     }
149: 
150:     // return the count of events for which the reminder has been send
151:     Ok(events.len())
152: }
153: 
154: /// send a reminder email for all bookings which are due with payment
155: pub(crate) async fn send_payment_reminders(
156:     pool: &PgPool,
157:     event_type: EventType,
158:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
159: ) -> Result<usize> {
160:     // get unpaid bookings and filter for bookings that are due with payment
161:     let bookings = super::get_unpaid_bookings(pool, event_type)
162:         .await?
163:         .into_iter()
164:         .filter(|booking| matches!(booking.due_in_days, Some(due_in_days) if due_in_days < 0))
165:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
166: 
167:     // prepare for message generation
168:     let email_account = email_sender
169:         .account(AccountLookup::ByType(event_type.into()))
170:         .await?;
171:     let message_type: MessageType = event_type.into();
172:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
173: 
174:     // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
175:     let subject = format!("{} Zahlungserinnerung", event_type.subject_prefix());
176:     let body = match event_type {
177:         EventType::Fitness => include_str!("../../../templates/payment_reminder_fitness.txt"),
178:         EventType::Events => include_str!("../../../templates/payment_reminder_events.txt"),
179:     };
180: 
181:     let mut event_cache = HashMap::new();
182:     for booking in bookings.iter() {
183:         // get the event from the cache of from the database
184:         let key = booking.event_id;
185:         if let Entry::Vacant(e) = event_cache.entry(key) {
186:             let value = db::get_event(pool, &booking.event_id, false)
187:                 .await?
188:                 .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' is missing", key))?;
189:             e.insert(value);
190:         }
191:         let event = event_cache
192:             .get(&key)
193:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' is not in the cache", key))?;
194: 
195:         // render the body for the email...
196:         let body = template::render_payment_reminder(body, event, booking)?;
197: 
198:         // ...and push the email into the messages list
199:         messages.push(
200:             Email::new(
201:                 message_type,
202:                 booking.email.clone(),
203:                 subject.clone(),
204:                 body,
205:                 None,
206:             )
207:             .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
208:         );
209:     }
210: 
211:     // send reminder emails to all bookings due with payment
212:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
213: 
214:     // mark payment reminder has been sent to the bookings
215:     // (to avoid duplicate sending of reminder emails)
216:     let booking_ids = bookings
217:         .into_iter()
218:         .map(|booking| booking.booking_id)
219:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
220:     db::mark_as_payment_reminder_sent(pool, &booking_ids).await?;
221: 
222:     Ok(booking_ids.len())
223: }
224: 
225: /// send participation confirmation after finished event
226: pub(crate) async fn send_participation_confirmation(
227:     pool: &PgPool,
228:     event_id: EventId,
229:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
230: ) -> Result<usize> {
231:     // fetch the event with all subscribers
232:     let mut event = db::get_event(pool, &event_id, true)
233:         .await?
234:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Error fetching event with id '{}'", event_id.get_ref()))?;
235:     let subscribers = event.subscribers.take().ok_or_else(|| {
236:         anyhow!(
237:             "Subscribers of event with id '{}' are missing",
238:             event_id.get_ref()
239:         )
240:     })?;
241: 
242:     let template = match event.event_type {
243:         EventType::Fitness => Ok(include_str!(
244:             "../../../templates/participation_confirmation_fitness.txt"
245:         )),
246:         EventType::Events => Err(anyhow!(
247:             "Participation confirmation is not supported for event type 'Events'."
248:         )),
249:     }?;
250: 
251:     let dates_len = event.dates.len();
252:     // abort if the event has no dates
253:     if dates_len < 1 {
254:         return Err(anyhow!(
255:             "Participation confirmation is not supported for an event without dates."
256:         ));
257:     }
258: 
259:     let fmt = "%d. %B %Y";
260:     let first_date = event
261:         .dates
262:         .first()
263:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' has no first date", event_id))?
264:         .format_localized(fmt, Locale::de_DE)
265:         .to_string();
266:     let last_date = event
267:         .dates
268:         .last()
269:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' has no last date", event_id))?
270:         .format_localized(fmt, Locale::de_DE)
271:         .to_string();
272:     let dates = format!("{dates_len} x {} Minuten", event.duration_in_minutes);
273: 
274:     // send an email per participant
275:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
276:     let subject = format!("{} Teilnahmebestätigung", event.subject_prefix());
277:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
278:     for subscriber in subscribers {
279:         if subscriber.enrolled {
280:             let price = subscriber.total_price(&event).to_euro();
281: 
282:             let bytes = export::create_participation_confirmation(
283:                 subscriber.first_name.clone(),
284:                 subscriber.last_name.clone(),
285:                 event.name.clone(),
286:                 first_date.clone(),
287:                 last_date.clone(),
288:                 price,
289:                 dates.clone(),
290:             )
291:             .await?;
292: 
293:             let body = template::render_participation_confirmation(template, &event, &subscriber)?;
294: 
295:             let message = email_sender
296:                 .build_message(&email_account)?
297:                 .to(subscriber.email.parse()?)
298:                 .subject(subject.clone())
299:                 .multipart(
300:                     MultiPart::mixed()
301:                         .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))
302:                         .singlepart(
303:                             Attachment::new(String::from("Teilnahmebestätigung.pdf"))
304:                                 .body(bytes, ContentType::parse("application/pdf")?),
305:                         ),
306:                 )?;
307: 
308:             messages.push(message)
309:         }
310:     }
311: 
312:     let count = messages.len();
313:     if count > 0 {
314:         email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
315:     }
316: 
317:     Ok(count)
318: }
319: 
320: fn create_prebooking_link(
321:     event_type: EventType,
322:     event_id: EventId,
323:     subscriber_id: i32,
324: ) -> Result<String> {
325:     let mut url = String::from("https://www.sv-eutingen.de/");
326:     url.push_str(match event_type {
327:         EventType::Fitness => "fitness",
328:         EventType::Events => "events",
329:     });
330:     url.push_str("?code=");
331: 
332:     // create the code
333:     url.push_str(&hashids::encode(&[
334:         event_id.into_inner().try_into()?,
335:         subscriber_id.try_into()?,
336:     ]));
337: 
338:     Ok(url)
339: }

1: use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
2: 
3: use lettre::Message;
4: 
5: use crate::email::{AccountLookup, MockEmailGateway};
6: use crate::models::{EmailAccount, EmailType};
7: 
8: /// Mock gateway that configures every lookup mode and a discarding
9: /// `send_messages`. Shorthand for `mock_email_gateway(accounts).0` — used by
10: /// tests that assert only that an email path fires, without inspecting
11: /// content.
12: pub(crate) fn mock_gateway_discarding(accounts: Vec<(EmailType, &str)>) -> MockEmailGateway {
13:     mock_email_gateway(accounts).0
14: }
15: 
16: /// Mock gateway where account-by-type lookup is expected exactly `times` and
17: /// sends are discarded. Used by tests that assert an email fires a bounded
18: /// number of times without inspecting content.
19: pub(crate) fn mock_gateway_times(
20:     accounts: Vec<(EmailType, &str)>,
21:     times: usize,
22: ) -> MockEmailGateway {
23:     let mut mock = MockEmailGateway::new();
24:     for (email_type, address) in accounts {
25:         let account = EmailAccount::new_for_test(email_type.clone(), address);
26:         let by_type = email_type.clone();
27:         mock.expect_account()
28:             .withf(move |lookup| matches!(lookup, AccountLookup::ByType(t) if t == &by_type))
29:             .times(times)
30:             .returning(move |_| {
31:                 let account = account.clone();
32:                 Box::pin(async move { Ok(account) })
33:             });
34:     }
35:     mock.expect_build_message().returning(|account| {
36:         Ok(lettre::Message::builder()
37:             .from(account.mailbox()?)
38:             .date_now())
39:     });
40:     mock.expect_send_messages()
41:         .returning(|_, _| Box::pin(async { Ok(()) }));
42:     mock
43: }
44: 
45: /// Canonical mock [`EmailGateway`]. Configures every lookup mode and a
46: /// capturing `send_messages` so each test gets:
47: ///   - account resolution (by type *and* by address, matching the `accounts`
48: ///     list, with the rest returning the configured address)
49: ///   - a real `MessageBuilder` from `build_message` so messages build offline
50: ///   - `send_messages` captures `(account, Vec<Message>)` per call
51: ///
52: /// Tests that don't care about captured messages simply ignore the returned
53: /// buffer. No-op cases (no expectations needed) use `MockEmailGateway::new()`
54: /// directly — the default mock panics on any call, which is the desired
55: /// "this path must not touch email" signal.
56: pub(crate) fn mock_email_gateway(
57:     accounts: Vec<(EmailType, &str)>,
58: ) -> (
59:     MockEmailGateway,
60:     Arc<Mutex<Vec<(EmailAccount, Vec<Message>)>>>,
61: ) {
62:     let mut mock = MockEmailGateway::new();
63: 
64:     for (email_type, address) in &accounts {
65:         let account = EmailAccount::new_for_test(email_type.clone(), address);
66:         let by_type = email_type.clone();
67:         mock.expect_account()
68:             .withf(move |lookup| matches!(lookup, AccountLookup::ByType(t) if t == &by_type))
69:             .returning(move |_| {
70:                 let account = account.clone();
71:                 Box::pin(async move { Ok(account) })
72:             });
73: 
74:         let account = EmailAccount::new_for_test(email_type.clone(), address);
75:         let by_address = address.to_string();
76:         mock.expect_account()
77:             .withf(move |lookup| matches!(lookup, AccountLookup::ByAddress(a) if a == &by_address))
78:             .returning(move |_| {
79:                 let account = account.clone();
80:                 Box::pin(async move { Ok(account) })
81:             });
82:     }
83: 
84:     mock.expect_build_message().returning(|account| {
85:         Ok(lettre::Message::builder()
86:             .from(account.mailbox()?)
87:             .date_now())
88:     });
89: 
90:     let captured: Arc<Mutex<Vec<(EmailAccount, Vec<Message>)>>> = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
91:     let for_send = captured.clone();
92:     mock.expect_send_messages()
93:         .returning(move |account, messages| {
94:             for_send.lock().unwrap().push((account.clone(), messages));
95:             Box::pin(async { Ok(()) })
96:         });
97: 
98:     (mock, captured)
99: }

(End of file - total 99 lines)
</content>

content.text:
# Standards-axis review — issue #77 (events::notifications extraction)

## Hard standard violations

None. The new module's `pub(crate)` visibility on the four `send_*` fns and the lifted `mock_gateway_*` helpers matches AGENTS.md's "pub(crate) for crate-wide items"; `create_prebooking_link` correctly stays module-private. `.await?`/`anyhow!`/`bail!` usage is consistent. Call-site rewrites (`api.rs:846`, `tasks.rs:27/49/68`) are mechanical `events::` → `events::notifications::` updates. `include_str!` paths correctly adjusted `../../` → `../../../`. No SQLx or DB-ID changes.

## Judgement-call smells (baseline)

**1. `bigdecimal::BigDecimal::from(20)` — Primitive Obsession / consistency drift.** `src/logic/events/notifications.rs:404-405, 442-443` (and the originals were imported `BigDecimal`). The rest of the codebase imports `BigDecimal` and writes `BigDecimal::from(20)`; the moved tests inline the full path five times:
```rust
price_member: Some(bigdecimal::BigDecimal::from(20)),
price_non_member: Some(bigdecimal::BigDecimal::from(25)),
```
The pre-move code had `use bigdecimal::BigDecimal;` at the parent `events.rs` test mod. The lift dropped the import without re-adding it; AGENTS.md's "BigDecimal for money" convention is satisfied in type but not in the project's customary spelling.

**2. Duplicated Code — test_utils.rs mocks.** `mock_gateway_times` (test_utils.rs:19-43) re-creates the `expect_build_message`/`expect_send_messages` returning closures verbatim from `mock_email_gateway` (test_utils.rs:84-97):
```rust
mock.expect_build_message().returning(|account| {
    Ok(lettre::Message::builder().from(account.mailbox()?).date_now())
});
```
The lift into `test_utils.rs` was the natural place to share those two closures; instead both mocks carry independent copies.

**3. Residual debug print — `notifications.rs:467-470`.** Moved verbatim, untouched smell:
```rust
let result = send_payment_reminders(&pool, EventType::Fitness, &mock_sender).await;
if let Err(e) = &result {
    eprintln!("Error: {:?}", e);
}
assert!(result.is_ok());
```
The `eprintln!` is dead-on-pass and the `if let Err` then `assert!(result.is_ok())` shape duplicates failure reporting that `assert!`'s panic already gives.

## Borderline (noting, not flagging)

- `notifications.rs:1-2` lists `use std::collections::HashMap;` and `use std::collections::hash_map::Entry;` as two statements; the convention elsewhere collapses to `use std::collections::{hash_map::Entry, HashMap};`. rustfmt accepted it, so cosmetic only.
- `notifications_integration_tests` (notifications.rs:373-376) splits the `crate::` group with a blank line before `use super::*;` — `super::*` is crate-relative, so it conceptually belongs in the same group per AGENTS.md's import-order rule.
